title: SigmaBlockedWalks
tex_block_html:
Let $G=(J,V,E,L)$ be a CDMG and $C \ins J \cup V$ a subset of nodes and $\pi$ a walk in $G$: \[ \pi =\lp v_0 \sus \cdots \sus v_n \rp. \] We say that the walk $\pi$ is:

  1. $C$-$\sigma$-open (or $\sigma$-open given $C$) if and only if:
    1. all colliders $v_k$ on $\pi$ are in $\Anc^G(C)$, and:
    2. all blockable non-colliders $v_k$ on $\pi$ are not in $C$.
  2. $C$-$\sigma$-blocked (or $\sigma$-blocked given $C$) if and only if:
    1. there exists a collider $v_k$ on $\pi$ that is not in $\Anc^G(C)$, or:
    2. there exists a blockable non-collider $v_k$ on $\pi$ in $C$.

Note that unblockable non-colliders are always $C$-$\sigma$-open, regardless of the subset $C \ins V \cup J$.

tex_statement.raw:
% Definition file for row def_3_17 -- "SigmaBlockedWalks".
% Auto-generated stub by scaffold/solve_chapter.py at row start. The
% manager agent (or a worker dispatched by it) fills the body below with the
% Lean-faithful definition statement(s). Stays close to the lecture notes.
%
% This file is a sibling of `main.tex` inside `<subsection>/tex/`. The
% `subfiles` package lets it compile both standalone (resolving its
% preamble via `main.tex`) and as part of `main.tex` via `\subfile{...}`.

\documentclass[main]{subfiles}

\begin{document}
% ref: def_3_17
% title: SigmaBlockedWalks
\def\rowref{def\_3\_17}
\phantomsection\label{def_3_17}

\begin{Def}[$\sigma$-blocked walks]\label{def:sigma_blocking}
    Let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG (def \ref{def-cdmg}); throughout we use the notation of def \ref{not-cdmg}, the family-relationships definition of def \ref{def_3_5}, the collider/non-collider machinery of def \ref{def:collider_noncollider}, and the blockable/unblockable refinement of def \ref{def:unblockable_noncollider}. Let $C \subseteq J \cup V$ be a subset of nodes, and let
    \[
        \pi = (v_0, a_0, v_1, a_1, \dots, v_{n-1}, a_{n-1}, v_n)
    \]
    be a walk in $G$ (def \ref{def:walks}, item~i.) for some integer $n \ge 0$, with $v_0, \dots, v_n \in J \cup V$ and $a_0, \dots, a_{n-1} \in E \cup L$ satisfying the walk constraint at every $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n - 1\}$:
    \[
        \bigl(a_k = (v_k, v_{k+1}) \;\land\; a_k \in E \cup L\bigr)
        \;\lor\;
        \bigl(a_k = (v_{k+1}, v_k) \;\land\; a_k \in E\bigr).
    \]

    \emph{Ancestor set of $C$.}
    The \emph{ancestor set} $\Anc^G(C) \subseteq J \cup V$ is the indexed union of the per-vertex ancestor sets of def \ref{def_3_5}, item~iv.:
    \[
        \Anc^G(C) := \bigcup_{c \in C} \Anc^G(c).
    \]
    Equivalently, for any $w \in J \cup V$,
    \[
        w \in \Anc^G(C) \quad\iff\quad \exists\, c \in C \text{ such that } w \in \Anc^G(c).
    \]
    In the clauses below we use $\Anc^G(C)$ in this union sense.

    \emph{$C$-$\sigma$-open walk.}
    The walk $\pi$ is said to be \emph{$C$-$\sigma$-open} (also called \emph{$\sigma$-open given $C$}) iff both of the following universal conditions hold:
    \begin{enumerate}[label=\roman*.)]
        \item for every position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$, if $k$ is a collider on $\pi$ in the sense of def \ref{def:collider_noncollider}, item~ii.\ (equivalently, $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k) = 2$), then $v_k \in \Anc^G(C)$;
        \item for every position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$, if $k$ is a blockable non-collider on $\pi$ in the sense of def \ref{def:unblockable_noncollider} (paragraph ``Blockable non-collider on $\pi$''), then $v_k \notin C$.
    \end{enumerate}
    Both clauses are universally quantified over the position index set $\{0, 1, \dots, n\}$; non-collider positions that are \emph{unblockable} (def \ref{def:unblockable_noncollider}, paragraph ``Unblockable non-collider on $\pi$'') are not constrained by clause~ii., and collider positions are not constrained by clause~ii.\ either (clause~i.\ handles them and clause~ii.\ is vacuous on collider positions since ``blockable non-collider'' carries ``non-collider'' as part of its precondition per def \ref{def:unblockable_noncollider}).

    \emph{$C$-$\sigma$-blocked walk.}
    The walk $\pi$ is said to be \emph{$C$-$\sigma$-blocked} (also called \emph{$\sigma$-blocked given $C$}) iff at least one of the following existential conditions holds:
    \begin{enumerate}[label=\roman*.)]
        \item there exists a position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$ such that $k$ is a collider on $\pi$ (def \ref{def:collider_noncollider}, item~ii.) and $v_k \notin \Anc^G(C)$;
        \item there exists a position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$ such that $k$ is a blockable non-collider on $\pi$ (def \ref{def:unblockable_noncollider}, paragraph ``Blockable non-collider on $\pi$'') and $v_k \in C$.
    \end{enumerate}

    \emph{De Morgan duality of the two classifications.}
    Inspecting the two clause-pairs above, clause~i.\ of ``$C$-$\sigma$-blocked'' is precisely the De Morgan negation of clause~i.\ of ``$C$-$\sigma$-open'' (``there exists a collider on $\pi$ with $v_k \notin \Anc^G(C)$'' is the negation of ``every collider on $\pi$ has $v_k \in \Anc^G(C)$''), and clause~ii.\ of ``$C$-$\sigma$-blocked'' is precisely the De Morgan negation of clause~ii.\ of ``$C$-$\sigma$-open'' (``there exists a blockable non-collider on $\pi$ with $v_k \in C$'' is the negation of ``every blockable non-collider on $\pi$ has $v_k \notin C$''). Disjoining the two negated clauses thus gives the negation of the conjunction of the two original clauses; consequently
    \[
        \pi \text{ is } C\text{-}\sigma\text{-blocked} \quad\iff\quad \pi \text{ is not } C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open},
    \]
    and the two notions are by construction mutually exclusive and jointly exhaustive over walks in $G$.

    \emph{Treatment of the trailing LN remark.}
    The LN source block (\texttt{graphs.tex}, \texttt{\textbackslash label\{def:sigma\_blocking\}}) follows the two definitions above with an embedded \texttt{claimmark} reading ``Note that unblockable non-colliders are always $C$-$\sigma$-open, regardless of the subset $C \subseteq J \cup V$.'' Per the row's authoritative \texttt{addition\_to\_the\_LN} clause \texttt{[claim\_type\_mismatch\_vertex\_vs\_walk]}, that remark is \emph{intentionally excluded} from the formalization of def \ref{def:sigma_blocking}: it is informal commentary, not part of the definition proper, and it applies the predicate ``$C$-$\sigma$-open'' to \emph{vertices} (specifically, to unblockable non-collider occurrences on a walk), whereas the formal definition above only ever introduces ``$C$-$\sigma$-open'' as a property of \emph{walks}. No Lean obligation is to be derived from the embedded remark within this definition. Any walk-level reformulation of its underlying content is handled separately as the dedicated claim row \texttt{claim\_3\_21}; the present definition treats the embedded remark as out of scope.
\end{Def}

\end{document}
tex_statement.html:
Let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG (def def-cdmg); throughout we use the notation of def not-cdmg, the family-relationships definition of def def_3_5, the collider/non-collider machinery of def def:collider_noncollider, and the blockable/unblockable refinement of def def:unblockable_noncollider. Let $C \subseteq J \cup V$ be a subset of nodes, and let \[ \pi = (v_0, a_0, v_1, a_1, \dots, v_{n-1}, a_{n-1}, v_n) \] be a walk in $G$ (def def:walks, item i.) for some integer $n \ge 0$, with $v_0, \dots, v_n \in J \cup V$ and $a_0, \dots, a_{n-1} \in E \cup L$ satisfying the walk constraint at every $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n - 1\}$: \[ \bigl(a_k = (v_k, v_{k+1}) \;\land\; a_k \in E \cup L\bigr) \;\lor\; \bigl(a_k = (v_{k+1}, v_k) \;\land\; a_k \in E\bigr). \]

Ancestor set of $C$. The ancestor set $\Anc^G(C) \subseteq J \cup V$ is the indexed union of the per-vertex ancestor sets of def def_3_5, item iv.: \[ \Anc^G(C) := \bigcup_{c \in C} \Anc^G(c). \] Equivalently, for any $w \in J \cup V$, \[ w \in \Anc^G(C) \quad\iff\quad \exists\, c \in C \text{ such that } w \in \Anc^G(c). \] In the clauses below we use $\Anc^G(C)$ in this union sense.

$C$-$\sigma$-open walk. The walk $\pi$ is said to be $C$-$\sigma$-open (also called $\sigma$-open given $C$) iff both of the following universal conditions hold:

  1. for every position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$, if $k$ is a collider on $\pi$ in the sense of def def:collider_noncollider, item ii.\ (equivalently, $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k) = 2$), then $v_k \in \Anc^G(C)$;
  2. for every position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$, if $k$ is a blockable non-collider on $\pi$ in the sense of def def:unblockable_noncollider (paragraph тАЬBlockable non-collider on $\pi$тАЭ), then $v_k \notin C$.

Both clauses are universally quantified over the position index set $\{0, 1, \dots, n\}$; non-collider positions that are unblockable (def def:unblockable_noncollider, paragraph тАЬUnblockable non-collider on $\pi$тАЭ) are not constrained by clause ii., and collider positions are not constrained by clause ii.\ either (clause i.\ handles them and clause ii.\ is vacuous on collider positions since тАЬblockable non-colliderтАЭ carries тАЬnon-colliderтАЭ as part of its precondition per def def:unblockable_noncollider).

$C$-$\sigma$-blocked walk. The walk $\pi$ is said to be $C$-$\sigma$-blocked (also called $\sigma$-blocked given $C$) iff at least one of the following existential conditions holds:

  1. there exists a position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$ such that $k$ is a collider on $\pi$ (def def:collider_noncollider, item ii.) and $v_k \notin \Anc^G(C)$;
  2. there exists a position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\}$ such that $k$ is a blockable non-collider on $\pi$ (def def:unblockable_noncollider, paragraph тАЬBlockable non-collider on $\pi$тАЭ) and $v_k \in C$.

De Morgan duality of the two classifications. Inspecting the two clause-pairs above, clause i.\ of тАЬ$C$-$\sigma$-blockedтАЭ is precisely the De Morgan negation of clause i.\ of тАЬ$C$-$\sigma$-openтАЭ (тАЬthere exists a collider on $\pi$ with $v_k \notin \Anc^G(C)$тАЭ is the negation of тАЬevery collider on $\pi$ has $v_k \in \Anc^G(C)$тАЭ), and clause ii.\ of тАЬ$C$-$\sigma$-blockedтАЭ is precisely the De Morgan negation of clause ii.\ of тАЬ$C$-$\sigma$-openтАЭ (тАЬthere exists a blockable non-collider on $\pi$ with $v_k \in C$тАЭ is the negation of тАЬevery blockable non-collider on $\pi$ has $v_k \notin C$тАЭ). Disjoining the two negated clauses thus gives the negation of the conjunction of the two original clauses; consequently \[ \pi \text{ is } C\text{-}\sigma\text{-blocked} \quad\iff\quad \pi \text{ is not } C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open}, \] and the two notions are by construction mutually exclusive and jointly exhaustive over walks in $G$.

Treatment of the trailing LN remark. The LN source block (graphs.tex, \texttt{\textbackslash label\{def:sigma\_blocking\}}) follows the two definitions above with an embedded claimmark reading тАЬNote that unblockable non-colliders are always $C$-$\sigma$-open, regardless of the subset $C \subseteq J \cup V$.тАЭ Per the row's authoritative addition_to_the_LN clause [claim_type_mismatch_vertex_vs_walk], that remark is intentionally excluded from the formalization of def def:sigma_blocking: it is informal commentary, not part of the definition proper, and it applies the predicate тАЬ$C$-$\sigma$-openтАЭ to vertices (specifically, to unblockable non-collider occurrences on a walk), whereas the formal definition above only ever introduces тАЬ$C$-$\sigma$-openтАЭ as a property of walks. No Lean obligation is to be derived from the embedded remark within this definition. Any walk-level reformulation of its underlying content is handled separately as the dedicated claim row claim_3_21; the present definition treats the embedded remark as out of scope.

lean_blocks.explanation: This is a `variable` declaration: Lean auto-binds `Node : Type*` and the instance `[DecidableEq Node]` as implicit arguments to every subsequent declaration in the surrounding `CDMG` namespace, sparing us from rewriting the same parameters on `IsSigmaOpenGiven` and `IsSigmaBlockedGiven`. The `Type*` notation makes `Node` universe-polymorphic, so callers can instantiate it at any universe level. The `DecidableEq Node` assumption is inherited from the chapter-wide convention: although the two predicates in this row are propositional and do not themselves run any decision procedure, they reach into `G.AncSet C` and `p.IsCollider k` / `p.IsBlockableNonCollider k`, which in turn unfold through `Finset.filter`, `Finset.image`, and option-valued list lookups on `p.vertices` тАФ all of which need decidable equality on the element type. Stronger structure on `Node` (e.g.\ `Fintype`, `LinearOrder`) is not required here and is deferred to downstream consumers that actually use it. Same convention as the rest of `Chapter3_GraphTheory`'s files.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- Auto-binds `Node` + `DecidableEq Node` as implicits; decidability is inherited by `G.AncSet`, `p.IsCollider`, `p.IsBlockableNonCollider` below.
variable {Node : Type*} [DecidableEq Node]

lean_blocks.explanation: A second `variable` line, scoped to the `Walk` sub-namespace, that auto-binds the ambient CDMG `G : CDMG Node` as an implicit argument to every subsequent declaration. Both `IsSigmaOpenGiven` and `IsSigmaBlockedGiven` take a walk `p : Walk G u v` whose graph parameter must come from somewhere; without this binder each signature would have to spell out `{G : CDMG Node}` explicitly. Lifting it once at the namespace top mirrors the LN's once-at-the-top quantifier "let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG" and keeps the bodies focused on the walk and the conditioning set. `G` is kept implicit because downstream callers reach into it via dot-notation on the walk (`p.IsSigmaOpenGiven C`) тАФ Lean recovers `G` from the type of `p`, so the user never types it. The two main predicates also call `G.AncSet C` on the bound `G`, so it must be in scope when their bodies type-check.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- Namespace-wide implicit `G : CDMG Node`; recovered from the walk's type via dot-notation at every use site.
variable {G : CDMG Node}

lean_blocks.code:
def IsSigmaOpenGiven {u v : Node} (p : Walk G u v) (C : Set Node) : Prop :=
  (тИА (k : тДХ) (vk : Node), p.vertices[k]? = some vk тЖТ p.IsCollider k тЖТ
      vk тИИ G.AncSet C) тИз
  (тИА (k : тДХ) (vk : Node), p.vertices[k]? = some vk тЖТ
      p.IsBlockableNonCollider k тЖТ vk тИЙ C)
lean_blocks.explanation: This block defines the LN's first classifier тАФ a walk $\pi$ is *$C$-$\sigma$-open* тАФ as a `Prop`-valued predicate on `(p : Walk G u v) (C : Set Node)`. The body is a conjunction of two parallel universals, one per LN clause. Each universal ranges over position indices `k : тДХ` together with the vertex `vk : Node` at that position, pinned down by the hypothesis `p.vertices[k]? = some vk`; this is the standard `getElem?` lookup that returns `none` past the end of the walk, so the LN's index range $k \in \{0, \dots, n\}$ is implicit тАФ out-of-range `k` falsify the antecedent and make the implication vacuous. Clause (i) says every collider position has its vertex inside the ancestor set $\Anc^G(C)$, written here as `G.AncSet C` (the indexed-union form from `def_3_5`). Clause (ii) says every *blockable* non-collider position has its vertex outside `C`; using `p.IsBlockableNonCollider k` rather than the broader `p.IsNonCollider k` is load-bearing тАФ unblockable non-collider positions are silently outside both clauses, matching the LN's intent. Dot-notation `p.IsSigmaOpenGiven C` reads as the LN's "$\pi$ is $C$-$\sigma$-open".
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- ref: def_3_17 (main, LN paragraph "C-╧Г-open walk") тАФ walk-level `Prop` predicate matching the LN's two-clause universal.
def IsSigmaOpenGiven {u v : Node} (p : Walk G u v) (C : Set Node) : Prop :=
  -- LN clause (i): universal over `(k, vk)` pairs; `getElem?` lookup implicitly bounds `k тЙд p.length`.
  (тИА (k : тДХ) (vk : Node), p.vertices[k]? = some vk тЖТ p.IsCollider k тЖТ
      -- Collider positions must have their vertex inside the indexed-union ancestor set `Anc^G(C)`.
      vk тИИ G.AncSet C) тИз
  -- LN clause (ii): same `(k, vk)` shape, restricted to *blockable* non-colliders only (unblockable positions silently excluded).
  (тИА (k : тДХ) (vk : Node), p.vertices[k]? = some vk тЖТ
      -- Blockable non-collider positions must have their vertex outside the conditioning set `C`.
      p.IsBlockableNonCollider k тЖТ vk тИЙ C)

lean_blocks.code:
def IsSigmaBlockedGiven {u v : Node} (p : Walk G u v) (C : Set Node) : Prop :=
  (тИГ (k : тДХ) (vk : Node),
      p.vertices[k]? = some vk тИз p.IsCollider k тИз vk тИЙ G.AncSet C) тИи
  (тИГ (k : тДХ) (vk : Node),
      p.vertices[k]? = some vk тИз p.IsBlockableNonCollider k тИз vk тИИ C)
lean_blocks.explanation: This block defines the LN's second classifier тАФ a walk $\pi$ is *$C$-$\sigma$-blocked* тАФ as the positive existential disjunction dual to `IsSigmaOpenGiven`, not as `┬м p.IsSigmaOpenGiven C`. The body is a `тИи` of two existentials, each pinning a position index `k : тДХ` and the vertex `vk` at that position via `p.vertices[k]? = some vk`. Disjunct (i) is a collider witness: some position `k` is a collider on `p` whose vertex `vk` escapes `G.AncSet C`. Disjunct (ii) is a blockable-non-collider witness: some position `k` is a blockable non-collider on `p` whose vertex `vk` lies in `C`. Encoding the predicate in this positive form lets downstream proofs build a blocking witness directly as `Or.inl тЯиk, vk, h_lookup, h_collider, h_ancтЯй` or `Or.inr тЯиk, vk, h_lookup, h_blockable, h_memтЯй`, instead of pushing a double negation through nested universals. The classical De Morgan equivalence `┬м p.IsSigmaOpenGiven C тЖФ p.IsSigmaBlockedGiven C` is left as a downstream lemma rather than a definitional reduction.
lean_blocks.code_annotated:
-- ref: def_3_17 (main, LN paragraph "C-╧Г-blocked walk") тАФ positive existential disjunction, NOT `┬м IsSigmaOpenGiven`.
def IsSigmaBlockedGiven {u v : Node} (p : Walk G u v) (C : Set Node) : Prop :=
  -- LN clause (i) dual: a collider witness whose vertex escapes the ancestor set `Anc^G(C)`.
  (тИГ (k : тДХ) (vk : Node),
      -- Triple-conjunct destructures cleanly as `тЯиh_lookup, h_collider, h_ancтЯй` at the use site.
      p.vertices[k]? = some vk тИз p.IsCollider k тИз vk тИЙ G.AncSet C) тИи
  -- LN clause (ii) dual: a *blockable* non-collider witness whose vertex lies in `C` (unblockable positions cannot witness).
  (тИГ (k : тДХ) (vk : Node),
      p.vertices[k]? = some vk тИз p.IsBlockableNonCollider k тИз vk тИИ C)

addition_to_the_LN: [claim_type_mismatch_vertex_vs_walk] The trailing claim asserting that unblockable non-colliders are always $C$-$\sigma$-open is excluded from the formalization. It is informal commentary, not part of Definition~\ref{def:sigma_blocking}, and no Lean obligation should be derived from it.
design_choices:
**Walk-level `Prop` predicates over `(p : Walk G u v) (C : Set Node)`.** The LN classifies *walks* relative to a conditioning set, so the predicates take the walk and the set directly and dot-notation `p.IsSigmaOpenGiven C` mirrors the LN's "$\pi$ is $C$-$\sigma$-open" writing. No vertex-level ╧Г-open predicate is introduced: the trailing LN remark that "unblockable non-colliders are always $C$-$\sigma$-open" applies the walk-level predicate to vertices, which is a type mismatch this row records but does not formalise; any walk-level reformulation of its underlying intent is left to a separate claim.

**PositionтАУvertex coupling via `p.vertices[k]? = some vk`.** The LN writes $v_k$ for the vertex at position $k$; the option-valued lookup pins down `vk` while implicitly bounding $k \le n$, since out-of-range lookups are `none`. The universal antecedents of `IsSigmaOpenGiven` then become vacuous on out-of-range positions, and the existential witnesses of `IsSigmaBlockedGiven` cannot be formed тАФ matching the LN's scoping to $\{0, \dots, n\}$ without an explicit upper-bound hypothesis. This mirrors the idiom already used in `def_3_15` and `def_3_16`.

**`p.IsBlockableNonCollider k`, not `p.IsNonCollider k`.** Clause (ii) of the LN restricts to *blockable* non-colliders specifically: unblockable positions do not constrain $C$-$\sigma$-openness, and they do not witness $C$-$\sigma$-blockedness either. The broader `IsNonCollider` would over-fire on the open side (forcing unblockable positions outside $C$) and under-fire on the blocked side (admitting unblockable positions in $C$ as blocking witnesses) тАФ neither of which the LN says. The restriction is the predicate-level realisation of the LN's intent.

**Positive existential disjunction for `IsSigmaBlockedGiven`, not `┬м IsSigmaOpenGiven`.** The LN writes $C$-$\sigma$-blocked as $\exists \dots \notin \Anc^G(C) \;\lor\; \exists \dots \in C$, the De Morgan dual of the open definition. Encoding it directly as that positive existential disjunction (rather than as a negation of `IsSigmaOpenGiven`) mirrors the LN's literal writing, lets downstream proofs construct a blocking witness as an immediate `Or.inl тЯиk, vk, _, _, _тЯй` term, and avoids pushing a double negation through nested universals and conjunctions every time a witness is needed. The equivalence `┬м p.IsSigmaOpenGiven C тЖФ p.IsSigmaBlockedGiven C` is a downstream (classical) De Morgan lemma to be proved when a row needs it, not a definitional reduction the def-shape forces.

**Two parallel universals/existentials, not a single quantifier over a combined predicate.** The LN spells each classification as two parallel clauses, so the formalisation matches: `IsSigmaOpenGiven` destructures as `тЯиh_collider, h_blockableтЯй` and `IsSigmaBlockedGiven` destructures into one of the two clause witnesses. A single quantifier over a sum-typed predicate would compile but would force every downstream proof to recover the bullet-list shape by hand.

**Reusing `G.AncSet` from `def_3_5`.** `CDMG.AncSet : CDMG Node тЖТ Set Node тЖТ Set Node` already encodes the indexed-union ancestor set $\Anc^G(C)$. Out-of-graph elements of $C$ contribute $G.\mathrm{Anc}\,c = \emptyset$ via the `w тИИ G` guard inside `Anc`, so the LN's $C \subseteq J \cup V$ hypothesis is not needed at the definition site тАФ an escaping $C$ simply restricts the effective ancestor set to $\Anc^G(C \cap (J \cup V))$. Downstream consumers that genuinely need the subset hypothesis pass it at the use site.
